title: epipactis helleborine subsp. tremolsii
intro: Epipactis helleborine subsp. tremolsii is a subspecies of the Helleborine orchid, a rare and endemic plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues, such as skin infections and respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of Epipactis helleborine subsp. tremolsii are said to provide health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures or infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and limited scientific research.
